html:
[1211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 早喪父母 - Means 'lost his parents at an early age,' a common phrase in classical literature to describe orphans.
  • 未有妻室 - Means 'had not yet married,' indicating the protagonist's marital status.
  • 綠衣雙鬟 - Describes a woman wearing green clothes and having two braids, a common description in classical literature.
  • 情愛甚至 - Means 'their feelings were very deep,' indicating the intensity of their relationship.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天水 - Refers to Tianshui, a city in modern-day Gansu Province, indicating the protagonist's origin.
  • 杭州錢塘 - Refers to Hangzhou, Qiantang, a city in modern-day Zhejiang Province, indicating the protagonist's destination.
  • 西湖葛嶺 - Refers to West Lake and Ge Ling, a famous scenic area in Hangzhou, providing geographical context.
  • 宋賈秋壑舊宅 - Refers to the old mansion of Jia Qiuhe, a prominent figure in the Southern Song Dynasty, providing historical context.
  • 巨室妾媵 - Refers to concubines of wealthy families, a common social phenomenon in classical literature.
  • 夜出私奔 - Refers to the act of running away secretly at night, a common theme in classical literature.
  • 事迹彰聞 - Refers to the fear of one's story becoming known, a common concern in classical literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 天水趙源 - Zhao Yuan from Tianshui, the protagonist.
  • 杭州錢塘 - Hangzhou, Qiantang, the protagonist's destination.
  • 西湖葛嶺 - West Lake and Ge Ling, the protagonist's residence.
  • 宋賈秋壑舊宅 - The old mansion of Jia Qiuhe, a historical reference.
  • 綠衣人 - The nickname of the woman, indicating her identity.

[1212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一夕 - Refers to 'one night,' setting the time frame for the event.
  • 被酒 - Means 'drunk' or 'intoxicated,' describing the state of the subject.
  • 戲謂 - Means 'jokingly said' or 'teasingly remarked,' indicating the tone of the statement.
  • 綠兮衣兮，綠衣黃裳 - A direct quotation from the *Shijing* (Book of Songs), *Green Robe*, a poem that uses the image of a green robe and yellow skirt to symbolize a harmonious and virtuous relationship.
  • 有慚色 - Means 'showed a look of shame,' describing the emotional reaction of the woman.
  • 數夕不至 - Means 'did not come for several nights,' indicating a period of absence.
  • 以婢妾待之 - Means 'treated as a servant or concubine,' expressing the woman's dissatisfaction with her status.
  • 扭促不安 - Means 'feel constrained and uneasy,' describing the emotional state of the woman.
  • 本欲相與郎君偕老 - Means 'originally wanted to live together with you until old age,' expressing the woman's initial intention.
  • 至情相感 - Means 'mutual affection and emotional connection,' describing the deep bond between the two.
  • 夙緣未盡 - Means 'the karmic connection from past lives is not yet exhausted,' suggesting a predestined relationship.
  • 得無相難乎 - Means 'am I not making it difficult for you?' expressing a sense of hesitation or concern.
  • 蓋其數當然 - Means 'it is fated to be so,' indicating that the situation is predetermined.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 綠兮衣兮，綠衣黃裳 - Quotation from the *Shijing* (Book of Songs), a classic Chinese poetry collection.
  • 宋平章秋壑 - Refers to Jia Sidao (1213–1275), a prominent statesman and chancellor during the Southern Song Dynasty, known for his influence and controversial policies.
  • 臨安 - Refers to Hangzhou, the capital of the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 良家子女 - Refers to girls from respectable families.
  • 少善弈棋 - Means 'was skilled at chess from a young age,' indicating the woman's talent.
  • 半閒堂 - Refers to a pavilion or hall, likely a place where Jia Sidao would entertain himself and others.
  • 蒼頭 - Refers to a male servant or attendant, often of a lower rank.
  • 煎茶 - Means 'to brew tea,' indicating the servant's duty.
  • 後堂 - Refers to the inner part of a house, typically reserved for private or intimate gatherings.
  • 美姿容 - Means 'beautiful appearance,' describing the servant's physical attractiveness.

Proper Nouns

  • 源 - The name of the male character, likely a servant or attendant.
  • 绿衣 - The name of the female character, referred to as 'green robe' due to her attire.
  • 宋平章秋壑 - Jia Sidao, the chancellor during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 临安 - Hangzhou, the capital of the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 半閒堂 - A pavilion or hall in the residence of Jia Sidao.
  • 苍头 - A male servant or attendant.
  • 后堂 - The inner part of a house, typically reserved for private or intimate gatherings.

[1213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 嘗以 - Classical phrase meaning 'once upon a time' or 'at one time,' used to introduce a past event.
  • 乘暗 - Literally 'taking advantage of the dark,' here it means 'in secret' or 'under the cover of darkness.'
  • 投君 - Means 'to give to you' or 'to present to you,' often used in the context of gifts or offerings.
  • 玳瑁指盒 - Refers to a box made of tortoiseshell, often used as a decorative or valuable gift.
  • 彼此雖各有意 - Means 'both of us had intentions' or 'both of us had feelings,' indicating mutual interest.
  • 而内外严密 - Means 'but the inner and outer were tightly sealed,' suggesting a lack of opportunity or means to act on those feelings.
  • 莫能得其便 - Means 'could not find a way to make it happen,' indicating the inability to act on their intentions.
  • 同辈所觉 - Means 'discovered by peers' or 'found out by colleagues.'
  • 谗于秋壑 - Means 'maliciously reported to Qiu He,' indicating that someone spoke ill of them to Qiu He.
  • 赐死 - Means 'executed by imperial decree' or 'ordered to commit suicide,' indicating a severe punishment.
  • 西湖断桥之下 - Refers to the Broken Bridge in West Lake, a famous location in Hangzhou, China.
  • 再世为人 - Means 'reborn in another life' or 'reincarnated as a human,' indicating a belief in reincarnation.
  • 儿犹在鬼录 - Means 'my child is still recorded in the Book of the Dead' or 'my child is still in the realm of the dead,' indicating that the child has died.
  • 得非命钦 - Means 'is this not fate?' or 'is this not destiny?' expressing a sense of inevitability.
  • 言讫 - Means 'after speaking' or 'upon finishing speaking,' indicating the end of a statement.
  • 呜咽泣下 - Means 'sobbed and wept,' indicating deep emotional distress.
  • 源亦为之动容 - Means 'Yuan was also moved,' indicating that Yuan was emotionally affected.
  • 久之 - Means 'after a long time' or 'eventually,' indicating a passage of time.
  • 审如此 - Means 'if indeed so' or 'if this is true,' indicating a conditional statement.
  • 再世因缘 - Means 'reincarnated karmic bond' or 'reincarnated relationship,' indicating a deep connection that persists across lifetimes.
  • 更加亲爱 - Means 'to love each other even more' or 'to become even closer,' indicating a deepening of affection.
  • 以偿畴昔之愿 - Means 'to fulfill past wishes' or 'to satisfy former desires,' indicating the desire to make up for lost time.
  • 素不善棋 - Means 'never good at chess' or 'not skilled in chess,' indicating a lack of proficiency.
  • 教之弈 - Means 'taught her chess' or 'instructed her in chess,' indicating the process of learning.
  • 尽得其妙 - Means 'completely mastered the skill' or 'fully understood the nuances,' indicating mastery.
  • 凡平日以棋称者 - Means 'all those who were known for their chess skills' or 'all those who were famous for their chess abilities,' indicating a group of skilled players.
  • 莫能敌也 - Means 'no one could defeat her' or 'no one could match her,' indicating exceptional skill.
  • 目击者 - Means 'witnesses' or 'those who saw it,' indicating firsthand observers.
  • 历历甚详 - Means 'clearly and in great detail' or 'very clearly and thoroughly,' indicating detailed recollection.
  • 贩盐数百艘至都市卖之 - Means 'sold salt carried by hundreds of boats to the city,' indicating a large-scale business operation.
  • 太学有诗曰 - Means 'a student at the Imperial Academy wrote a poem saying,' indicating a specific source of the poem.
  • 昨夜江头涌碧波 - Means 'last night, green waves surged at the riverbank,' describing a vivid scene.
  • 满船都载相公鹾 - Means 'the entire boat was loaded with salt for the officials,' indicating the cargo.
  • 虽然要作调羹用 - Means 'although it is needed for making soup,' indicating a practical use.
  • 未必调羹用许多 - Means 'perhaps not all of it is needed for making soup,' suggesting excess.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 绣罗钱箧 - Refers to a beautifully embroidered silk money box, a valuable and symbolic gift.
  • 玳瑁指盒 - Refers to a tortoiseshell finger box, another valuable and symbolic gift.
  • 秋壑 - Refers to Qiu He, a historical figure, possibly a high-ranking official or noble.
  • 西湖断桥 - Refers to the Broken Bridge in West Lake, a famous and romantic location in Hangzhou, China.
  • 再世为人 - Refers to the concept of reincarnation, a belief in many Eastern religions.
  • 鬼录 - Refers to the Book of the Dead, a concept in Chinese folklore and religion.
  • 太学 - Refers to the Imperial Academy, the highest institution of learning in ancient China.
  • 相公 - Refers to officials or scholars, often used in a respectful or formal context.
  • 鹾 - Refers to salt, a common commodity in ancient China.
  • 调羹 - Refers to a spoon, often used metaphorically to indicate a small amount of something.

Proper Nouns

  • 秋壑 - Qiu He, a historical figure.
  • 西湖断桥 - The Broken Bridge in West Lake, Hangzhou, China.
  • 太学 - The Imperial Academy, the highest institution of learning in ancient China.
  • 相公 - Officials or scholars, often used in a respectful or formal context.
  • 鹾 - Salt, a common commodity in ancient China.
  • 调羹 - Spoon, often used metaphorically to indicate a small amount of something.

[1214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遂以士人付獄 - 遂 means 'then' or 'thereupon,' and 付獄 means 'sent to prison.' Together, this phrase indicates that the scholar was sent to prison as a result of the previous action.
  • 論以诽谤罪 - 論 means 'charged with' or 'accused of,' and 诽谤罪 means 'the crime of slander.' This phrase indicates that the scholar was charged with the crime of slander.
  • 尝于浙西行公田法 - 尝 means 'once' or 'at one time,' 于 means 'in' or 'at,' and 行公田法 means 'implemented the public land system.' This phrase indicates that the person once implemented the public land system in the western part of Zhejiang.
  • 民受其苦 - 民 means 'the people,' and 受其苦 means 'suffered from it.' This phrase indicates that the people suffered from the implementation of the public land system.
  • 或题诗于路左 - 或 means 'some' or 'someone,' 题诗 means 'wrote a poem,' and 于路左 means 'on the left side of the road.' This phrase indicates that someone wrote a poem on the left side of the road.
  • 襄阳累岁困孤城 - 襄阳 is the name of a city, 累岁 means 'for many years,' 困 means 'trapped' or 'encircled,' and 孤城 means 'isolated city.' This phrase describes the situation where the city of Xiangyang has been trapped for many years in an isolated state.
  • 豢养湖山不出征 - 豢养 means 'nourished' or 'supported,' 湖山 means 'lakes and mountains,' and 不出征 means 'did not go out to fight.' This phrase suggests that the people were supported by the lakes and mountains but did not engage in military action.
  • 不识咽喉形势去 - 不识 means 'did not recognize,' 咽喉 means 'throat' or 'strategic point,' 形势 means 'situation' or 'terrain,' and 去 means 'to go' or 'to leave.' This phrase implies that the people did not understand the strategic importance of the area.
  • 公田枉自害苍生 - 公田 means 'public land,' 枉自 means 'in vain' or 'to no avail,' and 害苍生 means 'harm the common people.' This phrase criticizes the public land system for harming the common people without achieving its intended purpose.
  • 遭显戮 - 遭 means 'suffered,' and 显戮 means 'public execution.' This phrase indicates that the person was subjected to public execution.
  • 斋云水千人 - 斋 means 'fasting' or 'temple,' 云水 means 'clouds and water' (a metaphor for monks or hermits), and 千人 means 'a thousand people.' This phrase indicates that the person hosted a fasting event for a thousand monks or hermits.
  • 覆其钵于案而去 - 覆 means 'to cover' or 'to place,' 其钵 means 'his bowl,' 于案 means 'on the table,' and 而去 means 'and left.' This phrase describes the monk or hermit placing his bowl on the table and leaving.
  • 启于秋壑 - 启 means 'to inform' or 'to report,' and 于秋壑 means 'to Autumn Valley' (a person's name). This phrase indicates that the event was reported to Autumn Valley.
  • 得好休时便好休 - 得好休时 means 'when it is time to rest,' and 便好休 means 'it is good to rest.' This phrase advises that one should rest when it is appropriate.
  • 收花结子在绵州 - 收花 means 'to gather flowers,' 结子 means 'to bear fruit,' and 在绵州 means 'in Mianzhou.' This phrase suggests that one should gather flowers and bear fruit in Mianzhou.
  • 始知真仙降临而不识也 - 始知 means 'only then realized,' 真仙 means 'true immortal,' 降临 means 'descended,' and 而不识也 means 'but did not recognize it.' This phrase indicates that only then did they realize that a true immortal had descended but they did not recognize it.
  • 孰知有漳州木绵庵之厄也 - 孰知 means 'who would have known,' 漳州 is the name of a place, 木绵庵 means 'Muqian Temple,' and 之厄 means 'the disaster.' This phrase suggests that who would have known about the disaster at Muqian Temple in Zhangzhou.
  • 渔者张公获一鳖 - 渔者 means 'fisherman,' 张公 is the name of a person, and 获一鳖 means 'caught a turtle.' This phrase describes a fisherman named Zhang who caught a turtle.
  • 不三年而祸作 - 不三年 means 'within three years,' and 而祸作 means 'and disaster occurred.' This phrase indicates that disaster occurred within three years.
  • 盖物亦以先知数而不可逃也 - 盖 means 'indeed' or 'it is said,' 物 means 'things' or 'creatures,' 亦以先知数 means 'also because of prior knowledge of numbers,' and 而不可逃也 means 'and cannot be avoided.' This phrase suggests that indeed, things also because of prior knowledge of numbers cannot be avoided.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秋壑 - A historical figure, possibly a government official or scholar, whose name is 'Autumn Valley.'
  • 浙西 - Refers to the western part of Zhejiang Province, a region in China.
  • 公田法 - Refers to the public land system, a policy or practice involving the management of public lands.
  • 襄阳 - A city in Hubei Province, known for its historical significance.
  • 湖山 - Refers to lakes and mountains, often used metaphorically to describe natural resources or scenic beauty.
  • 咽喉形势 - Refers to strategic points or terrain, often used in military or political contexts.
  • 苍生 - Refers to the common people or the masses.
  • 云水 - A metaphor for monks or hermits, often used in Buddhist or Taoist contexts.
  • 绵州 - A place name, possibly referring to a city or region in China.
  • 漳州 - A place name, possibly referring to a city or region in China.
  • 木绵庵 - A temple or monastery, possibly located in Zhangzhou.
  • 张公 - A historical figure, possibly a fisherman or government official, whose name is 'Zhang.'

[1215]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 抑岂非数乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'is it not fate or destiny?'
  • 数至则散矣 - Means 'when the time comes, it will disperse.'
  • 因缘之契，夫妇之情 - Refers to the karmic bond and marital affection.
  • 荷蒙不弃，周旋许时 - Means 'to have been favored and treated kindly for a long time.'
  • 一念之私 - Means 'a single selfish thought.'
  • 蹈不测之祸 - Means 'to fall into unforeseen calamity.'
  • 海枯石烂，此恨难消 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'even if the seas dry up and the rocks crumble, this grudge will not be erased.'
  • 地老天荒，此情不泯 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'even if the earth grows old and the sky becomes desolate, this feeling will not fade.'
  • 续前生之好，践往世之盟 - Means 'to continue the good from a past life and fulfill an ancient vow.'
  • 面壁而卧，呼之不应矣 - Means 'to lie facing the wall, unresponsive to calls.'
  • 大伤恸 - Means 'deeply mournful.'
  • 虚葬于北山之麓 - Means 'to bury in a hollow grave on the northern slope of the mountain.'
  • 感其情，不复再娶 - Means 'moved by her feelings, he did not remarry again.'
  • 栖灵隐寺出家为僧 - Means 'to live in Lingyin Temple and become a monk.'
  • 终其身云 - Means 'and so he spent the rest of his life.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 数 - Refers to fate or destiny in Chinese philosophy.
  • 因缘之契 - A Buddhist concept of karmic connections.
  • 夫妇之情 - Refers to the marital bond, a significant theme in Chinese literature and culture.
  • 海枯石烂 - Hyperbolic expression used to convey eternal or unchanging conditions.
  • 地老天荒 - Hyperbolic expression used to convey eternal or unchanging conditions.
  • 灵隐寺 - A famous Buddhist temple in Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, known for its historical and cultural significance.

Proper Nouns

  • 灵隐寺 - The name of a specific Buddhist temple in Hangzhou.
  • 北山 - Refers to a specific mountain, likely in the context of the story's setting.

[1216]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 美風調 - Describes someone with good manners and demeanor.
  • 善吟咏 - Indicates skill in composing poetry.
  • 為众所推重 - Means 'highly respected by many.'
  • 素闻 - Means 'heard about' or 'knew about' from previous experience.
  • 思一游焉 - Means 'wanted to visit once.'
  • 遂以乡书赴荐 - Means 'then submitted his local examination results for recommendation.'
  • 侨居 - Means 'to live temporarily in a place.'
  • 无日不往来 - Means 'visited daily without fail.'
  • 足迹殆将遍焉 - Means 'his footsteps almost covered every place.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 延 - The era name of Emperor Ningzong of Song (1195-1224).
  • 永嘉 - A prefecture in Zhejiang province, known for its cultural significance.
  • 临安 - The capital of the Southern Song dynasty, now Hangzhou.
  • 科举 - The imperial examination system used to select government officials.
  • 乡书 - Local examination results.
  • 荐 - Recommendation for higher-level examinations.
  • 涌金门 - A famous gate in Hangzhou.
  • 灵隐、天竺、净慈、宝石 - Famous temples and scenic spots in Hangzhou.
  • 玉泉、虎跑、天龙、灵鹫 - Other notable places in Hangzhou.
  • 石屋之洞，冷泉之亭 - Specific scenic spots.
  • 幽涧深林，悬崖绝壁 - Natural landscapes typical of the region.

Proper Nouns

  • 延 - The era name of Emperor Ningzong of Song.
  • 永嘉 - A prefecture in Zhejiang province.
  • 滕穆 - The name of the protagonist.
  • 临安 - The capital of the Southern Song dynasty, now Hangzhou.
  • 涌金门 - A famous gate in Hangzhou.
  • 灵隐、天竺、净慈、宝石 - Famous temples and scenic spots in Hangzhou.
  • 玉泉、虎跑、天龙、灵鹫 - Other notable places in Hangzhou.
  • 石屋之洞，冷泉之亭 - Specific scenic spots.
  • 幽涧深林，悬崖绝壁 - Natural landscapes typical of the region.

[1217]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 七月之望 - Refers to the 15th day of the seventh lunar month, traditionally a full moon day, often associated with the Mid-Autumn Festival.
  • 荷香满身 - The fragrance of lotus flowers fills the air.
  • 生已大醉 - The person is already drunk.
  • 寝不能寐 - Unable to sleep.
  • 披衣而起 - Putting on clothes and getting up.
  • 延堤观望 - Wandering along the embankment to look around.
  • 信步而入 - Entering casually.
  • 宋亡已四十年 - The fall of the Song Dynasty has been forty years.
  • 园中台馆 - The pavilions and buildings in the garden.
  • 皆已颓毁 - All have fallen into ruin.
  • 惟瑶津西轩岿然独存 - Only the Yaojin West Pavilion remains standing alone.
  • 生至轩下，凭栏少憩 - The person went to the pavilion, leaned on the railing, and took a short rest.
  • 俄见一美人先行，一侍女随之 - Soon, a beautiful woman was seen leading the way, followed by a maid.
  • 风鬟云鬓，绰约多姿 - Her hair was like wind and clouds, her figure graceful and full of charm.
  • 殆若神仙 - Seemed almost like a fairy.
  • 生放逸者 - The person who is unrestrained and free-spirited.
  • 初见其貌，已不能定情 - At first seeing her appearance, already unable to control his feelings.
  • 及闻此作，技痒不可复禁 - Upon hearing this poem, his urge to write could no longer be restrained.
  • 即于轩下续吟曰 - Immediately, he continued to recite at the pavilion.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 曲院 - A place name, possibly referring to a specific area known for lotus flowers.
  • 雷峰塔 - Leifeng Pagoda, a famous pagoda in Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 聚景园 - Juying Garden, a historical garden in Hangzhou.
  • 会芳殿、清辉阁、翠光亭 - Specific names of pavilions and buildings in the Juying Garden.
  • 瑶津西轩 - Yaojin West Pavilion, a specific pavilion in the garden.
  • 宋亡 - The fall of the Song Dynasty, which occurred in 1279.
  • 《黍离》之悲 - A reference to the poem 'Shu Li' from the Book of Songs, expressing sorrow over the decline of a once-great civilization.
  • 湖山如故，风景不殊 - The lakes and mountains remain the same, but the scenery is unchanged, a common phrase expressing the contrast between the enduring landscape and the changing times.

Proper Nouns

  • 七月之望 - The 15th day of the seventh lunar month.
  • 曲院 - A place name.
  • 雷峰塔 - Leifeng Pagoda.
  • 聚景园 - Juying Garden.
  • 会芳殿 - Hui Fang Hall.
  • 清辉阁 - Qing Hui Pavilion.
  • 翠光亭 - Cui Guang Pavilion.
  • 瑶津西轩 - Yaojin West Pavilion.
  • 《黍离》 - A poem from the Book of Songs.
  • 湖山如故，风景不殊 - A common phrase expressing the contrast between the enduring landscape and the changing times.

[1218]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 湖上園亭好 - Describes a beautiful garden by the lake.
  • 相逢絕代人 - Means 'to meet someone of unparalleled beauty.'
  • 娥辭月殿 - Refers to the Moon Goddess Chang'e leaving the Moon Palace.
  • 織女下天津 - Refers to the Weaving Maid descending from the Milky Way.
  • 未會心中意 - Means 'does not understand the heart's intention.'
  • 渾疑夢里身 - Means 'feels as if in a dream.'
  • 願吹鄒子律 - Refers to the wish to blow the music of Zhu Zi (a legendary figure).
  • 幽谷發陽春 - Means 'to make spring bloom in a secluded valley.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 湖上園亭 - Refers to a garden by the lake, a common setting in classical Chinese literature.
  • 絕代人 - Refers to someone of extraordinary beauty.
  • 娥辭月殿 - Alludes to the legend of Chang'e, the Moon Goddess, who left the Moon Palace.
  • 織女下天津 - Alludes to the legend of the Weaving Maid, who descended from the Milky Way.
  • 鄒子律 - Refers to the music of Zhu Zi, a legendary figure known for his ability to control the weather.
  • 幽谷 - Refers to a secluded valley, often used in classical literature to symbolize isolation or seclusion.

Proper Nouns

  • 娥 - Refers to Chang'e, the Moon Goddess.
  • 織女 - Refers to the Weaving Maid, a character from the legend of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl.
  • 鄒子 - Refers to Zhu Zi, a legendary figure known for his ability to control the weather.

[1219]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 记前朝旧事 - Literally 'record the old events of the former dynasty,' introducing the theme of reminiscence and historical reflection.
  • 会神仙 - Literally 'meet immortals,' referring to a mystical or supernatural encounter.
  • 月地云阶 - A poetic phrase describing a celestial or ethereal setting, often associated with the moon and clouds.
  • 重携翠袖 - Literally 'again carrying the green sleeves,' a metaphor for a woman or her presence, often used to describe a graceful or elegant figure.
  • 来拾花钿 - Literally 'come to pick up the flower ornaments,' referring to the retrieval of decorative items, often symbolic of beauty or lost moments.
  • 繁花总随流水 - A metaphor for the transience of beauty and life, where flowers (beauty) are carried away by the stream (time).
  • 叹一场春梦杳难圆 - Literally 'lament a spring dream that is hard to fulfill,' expressing the futility or impossibility of certain desires or dreams.

  • Complex structures like '始也惧父母之责，故与君为卓氏之逃，盖出于不获已也' require breaking down into three parts: the initial fear, the action taken, and the reason for the action.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 妾文孝坊薛氏女，嫁平幼子康，不幸早卒，妾独蠕居 - This sentence introduces the speaker and her situation. The phrase '不幸早卒' (unfortunately died young) could be interpreted as either the speaker's husband or a previous family member.
  • 坐久，茶至再，洙辞出 - This sentence describes a sequence of events: sitting for a long time, tea being served twice, and Zhu declining to stay. The order of these actions is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the flow.
  • 美人留之曰 - This sentence introduces the beautiful lady's attempt to keep Zhu there. The phrase '留之' (kept him there) is a direct action.
  • 若盛东知君在此，而妾不能为一款曲，惶愧殊甚 - This sentence is a conditional statement about what would happen if Shengdong knew Zhu was there. The structure is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the conditional meaning.
  • 即陈酒馔，设二席，与洙耦坐 - This sentence describes the setup for the meal. The phrase '设二席' (arranged two tables) and '耦坐' (sat opposite) are key to understanding the arrangement.
  • 坐中劝酬极至，语杂谐谑 - This sentence describes the atmosphere during the meal. The phrase '劝酬极至' (urged to drink to the fullest extent) and '语杂谐谑' (conversation mixed with jokes and playful banter) are key.

  • 愿天子兮寿万春 - A wish for the emperor's longevity, with '寿万春' (live ten thousand springs) being a hyperbolic expression of eternal life.
  • 歌声小大，无异于人 - '无异于人' (no different from others) suggests that the singing is comparable to human voices.
  • 清彻绕越梁栋 - '清彻' (clear and pure) and '绕越梁栋' (echoing through beams and pillars) describe the quality and spread of the music.
  • 调契声谐 - '调契' (in harmony) and '声谐' (sounds are in accord) emphasize the perfect tuning and coordination of the music.
  • 举觞并劝曰 - '举觞' (raise the cup) and '并劝' (together encourage) describe the act of sharing a drink and offering congratulations.
  • 不德不足当雅贶 - '不德' (not worthy of) and '雅贶' (elegant gift) express humility in accepting the gift.
  • 献帝一紫螺壳 - '献帝' (present to the emperor) and '紫螺壳' (purple snail shell) describe the gift.
  • 是蛟龙髓 - '蛟龙髓' (dragon's marrow) is a mythical substance with special properties.
  • 以傅面，令人好颜色 - '以傅面' (apply to the face) and '令人好颜色' (make one look beautiful) describe the cosmetic use of the dragon's marrow.
  • 又女子在孕，产之必易 - '产之必易' (easier childbirth) describes the medicinal use of the dragon's marrow.
  • 何以此珠名洞穴珠 - '何以' (why) and '名' (named) ask for the reason behind the pearl's name.
  • 河底有一穴，深数百丈 - '河底' (river bottom) and '深数百丈' (hundreds of feet deep) describe the location and depth of the cave.
  • 中有赤蚌，蚌生珠 - '中有赤蚌' (contains red clams) and '蚌生珠' (clams produce pearls) describe the origin of the pearl.
  • 帝既深叹此事，又服朔之奇识 - '深叹此事' (deeply admire this matter) and '服朔之奇识' (admire the extraordinary knowledge of the scholar) express the emperor's appreciation.

  • 调契声谐 - Describes the perfect tuning and coordination of the music, reflecting the importance of harmony in Chinese aesthetics.
  • 举觞并劝曰 - Describes the act of sharing a drink and offering congratulations, reflecting the social and ceremonial aspects of the event.
  • 不德不足当雅贶 - Expresses humility in accepting the gift, reflecting Confucian values of modesty.

  • 是蛟龙髓 - References the mythical substance of dragon's marrow, which has special properties.
  • 以傅面，令人好颜色 - Describes the cosmetic use of the dragon's marrow, reflecting the importance of appearance in Chinese culture.
  • 又女子在孕，产之必易 - Describes the medicinal use of the dragon's marrow, reflecting the importance of health and well-being in Chinese culture.
  • 何以此珠名洞穴珠 - Asks for the reason behind the pearl's name, reflecting the curiosity and interest in natural phenomena.
  • 河底有一穴，深数百丈 - Describes the location and depth of the cave, reflecting the importance of natural wonders in Chinese culture.
  • 中有赤蚌，蚌生珠 - Describes the origin of the pearl, reflecting the importance of nature and its resources in Chinese culture.
  • 帝既深叹此事，又服朔之奇识 - Expresses the emperor's appreciation for the extraordinary knowledge of the scholar, reflecting the value placed on wisdom and learning in Chinese culture.

[1270]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 花吐嫩红初着雨 - Flowers bloom with tender red, first touched by rain. Evokes beauty and delicacy, with rain symbolizing a gentle, nurturing force.
  • 絮飘轻白细惟风 - Fluff drifts lightly white, fine and only the wind. Describes the soft, airy quality of fluff, emphasizing the wind's role in its movement.
  • 霞舒锦练光凝岭 - The morning glow spreads like brocade, its light fixed on the mountain. Metaphor comparing the sunrise to a luxurious fabric, with the mountain as the canvas.
  • 月上圆盘影挂松 - The moon rises like a round plate, its shadow hangs on the pine. Vividly describes the moon's shape and its reflection on a pine tree.
  • 凉风扇透朝肌冷 - Cool breeze penetrates the morning skin, bringing cold. Describes the sensation of a cool breeze in the morning, emphasizing its penetrating chill.
  • 骤雨盆倾夜帐寒 - Sudden rain pours like a basin, chilling the night curtain. Uses the metaphor of rain pouring from a basin to convey the intensity and coldness of a sudden downpour.
  • 香栋出飞新燕小 - The fragrant beams see small new swallows fly out. Describes the sight of young swallows emerging from a fragrant structure, evoking renewal and life.
  • 翠池盈贴嫩荷团 - The green pool is full, with tender lotus clusters pressed together. Vividly describes a lush, vibrant pool filled with lotus flowers.
  • 芦岸宿鸠秋寂寂 - By the reed bank, the turtledove roosts, autumn silent. Evokes a serene, quiet autumn scene with the turtledove as a symbol of calmness.
  • 桂庭飞蝶晚苍苍 - In the orchid courtyard, butterflies fly, evening gray. Describes a late afternoon scene in an orchid-filled courtyard, with butterflies adding life to the fading light.
  • 孤灯剪尽挨长夜 - The lone lamp burns through the long night. Conveys the loneliness and endurance of a long, solitary night.
  • 独枕愁思梦远乡 - Alone in bed, filled with longing, dreaming of a distant home. Expresses deep homesickness and the struggle to sleep.
  • 天冷夜清霜满野 - The cold night is clear, frost covers the field. Describes a crisp, frosty night, emphasizing the cold and clarity of the scene.
  • 月寒凤凛雪迷城 - The moon is cold, the phoenix's breath is chilling, snow obscures the city. Uses poetic imagery to convey the harshness of a snowy night, with the moon and phoenix symbolizing coldness and mystery.
  • 鲜红烛影深闺静 - The bright red candlelight casts shadows in the secluded chamber. Describes a quiet, intimate scene in a dimly lit room, with the candlelight adding warmth and mystery.
  • 淡白梅香暗阁清 - The faint white plum fragrance fills the dim pavilion. Evokes a serene, fragrant atmosphere in a quiet, dimly lit space.
  • 是夜就寝，极尽幽欢。 - That night, they slept, enjoying the utmost intimacy. Describes a night of deep, intimate connection.
  • 大才惊讶失色，举身流汗。促舟还家，遂得疑疾。梦中，尝见美人与之同榻，联诗数篇，不及备述。 - The great talent was shocked, his face pale, sweating profusely. He hurriedly returned home in a boat, and thus fell ill with doubt. In his dreams, he often saw the beautiful woman sharing his bed, composing several poems together, too many to recount. Describes the aftermath of the encounter, including shock, illness, and recurring dreams.

  • 古词 - Ancient poem, often with a dreamlike or supernatural quality.

[1285]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 上马腾腾而行 - Vivid phrase meaning 'to mount the horse and gallop swiftly,' conveying a sense of urgency and speed.
  • 倏忽渐高 - Means 'suddenly rising higher and higher,' emphasizing the rapid ascent.
  • 但讶其稳疾 - Means 'only amazed at its stability and speed,' highlighting the rider's surprise at the smoothness of the ride.
  • 不自知其云上也 - Means 'not realizing that one is ascending into the clouds,' indicating the rider's unawareness of the supernatural nature of the journey.
  • 风急如箭 - Simile comparing the speed of the wind to an arrow, emphasizing its intensity.
  • 雷霆起于步下 - Means 'thunder rising from below,' suggesting a powerful and awe-inspiring natural phenomenon.
  • 随所跃，辄滴之 - Means 'as it leaps, it drops,' referring to the horse's movements and the resulting raindrops.
  • 电掣云开 - Vivid phrase meaning 'lightning streaks through the clouds,' describing a sudden and dramatic clearing of the sky.
  • 下见所憩村 - Means 'seeing the village where one had rested below,' indicating the rider's view from above.
  • 吾扰此村多矣 - Means 'I have troubled this village many times,' expressing regret for past actions.
  • 方德其人，计无以报 - Means 'now I value the people of the village, but I have no way to repay them,' showing the rider's sense of indebtedness.
  • 今久旱，苗稼将悴 - Means 'now there has been a long drought, and the crops are withering,' describing the current situation in the village.
  • 而雨在我手，宁复惜之 - Means 'but the rain is in my hand, so how could I be stingy with it?' expressing the rider's resolve to help.
  • 顾一滴不足濡 - Means 'but one drop is not enough to moisten,' indicating the need for more rain.
  • 乃连下二十滴 - Means 'so he let down twenty drops,' showing the rider's action to provide sufficient rain.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 上马腾腾而行 - Reflects the traditional Chinese practice of horseback riding, often associated with travel and adventure.
  • 雷霆起于步下 - References the Chinese concept of natural phenomena as manifestations of divine power.
  • 随所跃，辄滴之 - Suggests a supernatural ability to control the weather, a common theme in Chinese folklore and mythology.
  • 电掣云开 - Describes a dramatic and awe-inspiring natural event, often used in classical Chinese literature to convey a sense of grandeur.
  • 吾扰此村多矣 - Reflects the Chinese cultural value of self-reflection and the importance of making amends for past wrongs.
  • 方德其人，计无以报 - Highlights the Chinese concept of gratitude and the desire to reciprocate kindness.
  • 今久旱，苗稼将悴 - Reflects the agricultural context of ancient China, where droughts were a significant concern.
  • 而雨在我手，宁复惜之 - Expresses the Chinese value of generosity and the willingness to help others in need.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sequence '上马腾腾而行，倏忽渐高，但讶其稳疾，不自知其云上也' must be read as a continuous description of the rider's experience, emphasizing the sudden and rapid ascent.
  • The elliptical phrase '随所跃，辄滴之' must be understood as the horse's movements causing raindrops to fall.
  • The sequence '电掣云开，下见所憩村' describes a sudden change in the weather and the rider's view from above.
  • The sequence '吾扰此村多矣，方德其人，计无以报' expresses the rider's regret and sense of indebtedness.
  • The sequence '今久旱，苗稼将悴，而雨在我手，宁复惜之' describes the current situation and the rider's resolve to help.
  • The sequence '顾一滴不足濡，乃连下二十滴' shows the rider's action to provide sufficient rain.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 相 - Refers to the position of chancellor, the highest civil official in the imperial court.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '及明，望其村，水已極目' describes the scene at dawn, where the village is barely visible due to the vast expanse of water.
  • The clause '竟以兵權靖寇難，功蓋天下' succinctly summarizes the person's military achievements and their impact.
  • The phrase '終不及於相' indicates the ultimate failure to reach the highest civil office.
  • The proverb '關東出相，關西出將' is used to explain the regional distribution of civil and military leadership.
  • The clause '所以言奴者，亦下之象' explains the symbolic use of servants to represent lower status.
  • The hypothetical statement '向使二奴皆取，即極將相矣' suggests that if the right servants were chosen, the person would have achieved the highest positions of general and chancellor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秦之役夫 - A laborer in the Qin state. This refers to the period of the Qin dynasty (221–206 BCE), known for its harsh conscription and labor policies.
  • 始皇帝 - The First Emperor of Qin, Qin Shi Huang, known for his centralization of power, construction of the Great Wall, and pursuit of immortality.
  • 徐福 - Xu Fu, a legendary figure in Chinese history and folklore. According to tradition, he was a Daoist alchemist and scholar who was sent by Qin Shi Huang to find the elixir of immortality and the fabled islands of the immortals.
  • 童男童女千人 - Refers to the historical account of Qin Shi Huang sending Xu Fu with a group of 3,000 boys and girls to find the elixir of immortality, though this number may be exaggerated.
  • 海岛 - 'Island(s)' likely refers to the mythical Penglai, Fangzhang, and Yingzhou, the three islands of the immortals in Daoist cosmology.
  • 石桥 - A reference to the mythical bridges of the immortals, often associated with Daoist and Buddhist legends.
  • 蓬岫 - A poetic term for the mythical island of Penglai, known for its misty mountains and immortals.
  • 始皇煨烬典坟 - Refers to the historical event known as the 'Burning of Books and Burying of Scholars,' in which Qin Shi Huang destroyed Confucian texts and executed scholars.
  • 坑杀儒士 - The same event as above, where Confucian scholars were buried alive.
  • 缙绅 - The scholar-official class, often associated with Confucian ideals.
  • 簪绂 - The hairpin and ribbon worn by officials, symbolizing their rank and status.

Proper Nouns

  • 始皇帝 - The First Emperor of Qin, Qin Shi Huang.
  • 徐福 - Xu Fu, a legendary figure in Chinese history and folklore.
  • 秦 - The Qin state, later the Qin dynasty (221–206 BCE).
  • 蓬岫 - A poetic term for the mythical island of Penglai.
  • 陶君 - Likely the name of one of the two men, possibly 'Tao Jun.'

  • 金殿不胜秋，月斜石楼冷 - Poetic description of a scene where the golden palace cannot withstand the autumn, and the moon is slanted over a cold stone tower. Conveys a sense of desolation and melancholy.
  • 谁是相顾人，褰帷吊孤影 - Rhetorical question and metaphorical expression meaning 'Who is the person looking at each other? Who is the one lifting the curtain to mourn the lonely shadow?' Suggests a sense of isolation and introspection.
  • 非鬼物乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Is it not a ghost?' Expresses doubt about the nature of the singer.
  • 又非狐狸乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Is it not a fox?' Expresses doubt about the nature of the singer, suggesting it might be a cunning or deceitful entity.
  • 吾闻，魂气升于天，形魄归于地，是无质矣，何鬼之有 - Statement meaning 'I have heard that the spirit rises to heaven, and the body returns to earth. Therefore, there is no substance, so how can there be a ghost?' Argues that the singer cannot be a ghost because it lacks a physical form.
  • 佞人也 - Term meaning 'a cunning or deceitful person.' Describes the nature of a fox.
  • 一中其媚，祸必能及 - Statement meaning 'If one falls for its charm, disaster will surely follow.' Warns against the dangers of being deceived by a cunning entity.
  • 其世业功德，实利生民 - Statement meaning 'Its family's virtue and merit truly benefit the people.' Argues that the singer's lineage has done good for humanity.
  • 某虽不淑，焉能苟媚而欲奉祸乎 - Statement meaning 'Although I am not virtuous, how could I flatter and bring disaster?' Expresses the singer's refusal to be deceitful.
  • 可闻姓氏否 - Question meaning 'Can I know your surname and given name?' Asks for the singer's identity.
  • 燧人氏之苗裔也 - Statement meaning 'I am a descendant of Suiren.' Identifies the singer's lineage.
  • 始祖有功烈于人，乃统丙丁镇南方 - Statement meaning 'Our ancestor had great achievements for humanity and was appointed to govern the south.' Describes the ancestor's accomplishments and role.
  • 复以德王 - Statement meaning 'He was then made king by virtue.' Describes the ancestor's elevation to a position of power.
  • 神农陶唐氏，后又王于西汉 - Statement meaning 'Shennong and Taotang, later became kings in the Western Han dynasty.' Describes the lineage's connection to historical figures.
  • 因食采于宋，远祖无忌，以威猛暴耗，人不可亲，遂为白泽氏所执 - Statement meaning 'Because of their fief in Song, our distant ancestor Wuji, due to his fierce and violent nature, was not approachable and was captured by Bai Ze.' Describes the ancestor's downfall.
  • 汉明帝时，佛法东流 - Statement meaning 'During the reign of Emperor Ming of Han, Buddhism spread to the East.' Provides historical context.
  • 摩竺法，兰二罗汉，奏请某十四代祖，令显扬释教 - Statement meaning 'Two Arhats, Mofu and Lan, petitioned for my fourteenth-generation ancestor to promote Buddhism.' Describes the ancestor's role in spreading Buddhism.
  • 魏武季年灭佛法、诛道士，而长明公幽死 - Statement meaning 'In the later years of Cao Cao, he suppressed Buddhism and killed Daoists, and the Longming Gong died in obscurity.' Describes the persecution of Buddhism.
  • 魏文嗣位，佛法重兴，复以长明世子袭之 - Statement meaning 'When Wei Wen ascended the throne, Buddhism revived, and the title of Longming Gong was inherited by his son.' Describes the revival of Buddhism.

  • 当于飞之秋，不当令东幢岿然无偶 - In the season of flying (i.e., the spring), it would be inappropriate for the eastern pavilion to stand alone without a counterpart, a metaphorical statement about symmetry or balance.
  • 帝即命立西幢，遂封某为西明夫人 - The emperor immediately ordered the construction of the western pavilion and then enshrined someone as the Western Bright Lady, indicating a ritual or title bestowment.
  • 因赐玻珀膏，润于肌肤；设珊瑚帐，固予形貌 - He then bestowed glass pearl ointment to nourish her skin; he set up a coral canopy to preserve her appearance, using luxurious materials for symbolic or actual beauty.
  • 于是巽生及蛾，不复强暴矣 - Thus, the wind-born and the moth no longer acted aggressively, metaphorical references to natural forces or entities.

  • 非不能也，盖承先祖之明德，禀炎上之烈性 - It is not that they cannot, but they inherit the virtue of their ancestors and possess the fierce nature of fire rising upward, indicating a cause for their abilities.
  • 奸声乱色，不置于心 - Deceitful sounds and colors do not enter the heart, suggesting moral purity or resistance to corruption.
  • 其所能者，大则铄金为五兵、为鼎鼐钟镛，小则花食为百品 - Their abilities are such that they can melt gold into weapons, into cauldrons, bells, and chimes; on a smaller scale, they can transform flowers into a hundred delicacies, a metaphor for transformation and creation.
  • 为炮燔烹炙 - They can be used for roasting, boiling, and grilling, suggesting the use of fire in transformation.
  • 动即煨山岳而烬原野，静则烛幽暗而破昏蒙 - When active, they can burn mountains and scorch the plains; when still, they can illuminate darkness and dispel confusion, a metaphor for power and illumination.
  • 抚朱弦、吹玉管、骋纤腰、矜皓齿 - To pluck the red strings, to blow the jade pipes, to display a slender waist, to show white teeth, describing the physical and artistic aspects of beauty.
  • 皆冶容之未事，是不为也 - All of these are minor aspects of beauty, and thus are not pursued, indicating a higher moral or spiritual focus.
  • 昨闻足下有幽隐之志，籍甚既久，愿一款颜，由斯而来，非敢自献 - I heard that you have long harbored a secluded and hidden aspiration; I wish to see your face once, and thus come to you, not daring to offer myself, a respectful and humble approach.
  • 宵清月朗，喜觌良人，桑中之讥，亦不能耻 - On a clear night with bright moon, I am happy to meet a good man; even the criticism of 'Sangzhong' (a reference to clandestine meetings) cannot make me ashamed, alluding to the Song of Songs or romantic encounters.
  • 倘运与时会，少承周旋，必无累于盛德 - If fate and opportunity align, and if you allow me a little companionship, I will certainly not tarnish your great virtue, a polite request for association.
  • 稹拜而纳之 - Zhen bowed and accepted her, indicating acceptance of a proposal or relationship.

  • 尝遇风雨，有婴儿送红裳诗，其词云 - Once, during a storm, an infant sent a red robe poem, its words being..., indicating a poetic or symbolic event.

  • 搜诸宫人之美者，辄幽之别室，系以大械 - She would search for beautiful palace women and then confine them in separate rooms, chaining them with large shackles. This describes Cui's cruel behavior.
  • 刻木为人手，击其颊，又以铁锥刺其臂 - She would carve wooden hands to strike their cheeks and then use iron spikes to pierce their arms. This further illustrates Cui's cruelty.
  • 一岁中死者八十四人 - Eighty-four people died in one year. This indicates the number of victims.
  • 金凤已乞身为尼，深自匿，故得免 - Jin Feng had already begged to become a nun, hiding deeply, and thus escaped. This explains how Jin Feng avoided Cui's cruelty.
  • 次年，延翰为周彦琛所杀，而延钧立 - The following year, Yan Han was killed by Zhou Yanchen, and Yan Jun took the throne. This describes the change in power.
  • 延钧，审知次子 - Yan Jun, the second son of Shen Zhi. This identifies Yan Jun's lineage.

  • 后宫数百，无可意者 - There were hundreds of concubines, none of whom pleased him. This indicates Yan Jun's dissatisfaction with his concubines.
  • 内侍李仿，极誉金凤姿色超绝 - The eunuch Li Fang highly praised Jin Feng's extraordinary beauty. This highlights Jin Feng's beauty.
  • 延钧御紫宸门，宣见，大悦，封之为淑妃 - Yan Jun held court at the Zichen Gate, summoned her, and was greatly pleased, appointing her as the Virtuous Consort. This describes Yan Jun's reaction to Jin Feng.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 李仿知人心罪己，不自安，私与春燕画全身之策 - Complex sentence meaning 'Li Fang, knowing the people's feelings and blaming himself, felt uneasy and privately plotted with Chunyan to devise a complete plan.'
  • 以太子继鹏与匡胜有隙，乃言春燕之美于继鹏 - Sentence meaning 'since Crown Prince Jipeng had a rift with Kuang Sheng, he spoke of Chunyan's beauty to Jipeng.'
  • 继鹏入宫问病，遇春燕于前庑，悦之，就所居焉 - Sentence meaning 'Jipeng entered the palace to inquire about her illness, met Chunyan in the front hall, was pleased, and cohabited with her.'
  • 匡胜闻而白其事，延钧大怒，与次子继韬议杀继鹏 - Sentence meaning 'Kuang Sheng heard and reported the matter, and Yan Jun became extremely angry, discussing with his second son Jitao the killing of Jipeng.'

  • 仿遽令壮士先杀李可殷于家，质明，金凤诉之 - Sentence meaning 'Fang hastily ordered a strong man to kill Li Keyin at home, and at dawn, Jin Feng reported it.'

  • 会亲里，携家之官 - Means 'he gathered relatives and moved his family to take up office,' indicating the social and familial aspects of his career advancement.
  • 膺翟之封 - Means 'he received the honorific title of 'Zhi' (翟),' which was a title of nobility or honor.
  • 遐迩传播，以为奇遇云 - Means 'the story spread far and wide, and people regarded it as a remarkable fortune,' emphasizing the widespread admiration and recognition of his success.
  • 大历中 - Refers to the Dali era, a period during the Tang Dynasty (766-779 AD).
  • 小字试莺 - Means 'her nickname was Shiying,' a common practice in ancient China to give a person a more personal or affectionate name.
  • 翰墨 - Refers to writing and literature, often used to describe someone skilled in calligraphy and poetry.
  • 丰姿艳体 - Means 'graceful and beautiful appearance,' describing her physical attractiveness.
  • 隐于谷里，显于澧浔 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'hidden in the valleys, revealed in the rivers,' suggesting her beauty is both modest and striking.
  • 贵比于白玉，重匹于黄金 - Means 'as precious as white jade, as heavy as gold,' emphasizing her value and beauty.
  • 既入燕姬之梦，还鸣宋玉之琴 - A literary allusion referring to the beauty of the woman in the dream of Yan Ji and the music of Song Yu, both famous for their poetic and musical talents.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乡荐 - Refers to the recommendation system for the imperial examinations, where local officials would recommend candidates to take the higher-level exams.
  • 进士 - The highest degree in the imperial examination system, awarded to those who passed the final examination.
  • 翁姑 - Refers to the husband's parents, specifically the father-in-law and mother-in-law.

  • 遐迩传播 - Means 'spread far and wide,' indicating the widespread nature of the news or story.
  • 大历中 - The Dali era was a period of relative peace and cultural flourishing during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 小字试莺 - The nickname 'Shiying' (试莺) suggests a delicate and graceful nature, often associated with young women.
  • 翰墨 - In ancient Chinese culture, calligraphy and poetry were highly esteemed arts, and being skilled in them was a mark of refinement.
  • 丰姿艳体 - Physical beauty was highly valued in ancient China, and this phrase emphasizes the woman's attractiveness.
  • 隐于谷里，显于澧浔 - This metaphorical expression is often used to describe the contrast between hidden virtue and public recognition.
  • 贵比于白玉，重匹于黄金 - These comparisons highlight the woman's exceptional beauty and value, using precious materials to convey her worth.
  • 既入燕姬之梦，还鸣宋玉之琴 - These allusions to Yan Ji and Song Yu are literary references to famous beauties and poets, adding a layer of cultural significance to the description of the woman.

  • 晁采 - The name of the woman, with her nickname 'Shiying.'
  • 大历 - The era name of the Tang Dynasty, indicating the time period.
  • 燕姬 - A reference to a famous beauty in Chinese literature.
  • 宋玉 - A famous poet and statesman in Chinese history, known for his literary works and musical talents.

  • 一集佳人白玉钗 - "一集" means to gather or come together, "佳人" refers to a beautiful woman, and "白玉钗" is a white jade hairpin. The phrase suggests that the phoenix would gather around a beautiful woman with a white jade hairpin.

  • 茂持啖未竟，坠一子于盆水中 - "茂持啖" means Mao holding and eating, "未竟" means not yet finished, and "坠一子于盆水中" means dropping one seed into the water. The phrase describes the action of eating and dropping a seed.
  • 有喜鹊过，恶污其上，茂遂弃之 - "有喜鹊过" means a magpie passing by, "恶污其上" means disliking the dirt on it, and "茂遂弃之" means Mao then discarded it. The phrase describes the reaction to the dropped seed being soiled by a magpie.
  • 明早，有并蒂花开于水面，如梅英大 - "明早" means the next morning, "有并蒂花开于水面" means there were twin blossoms opening on the water surface, and "如梅英大" means as large as plum blossoms. The phrase describes the appearance of twin flowers on the water.
  • 吾事济矣 - "吾事" means my affairs, and "济矣" means are successful. The phrase expresses that the sender's affairs are now successful.
  • 取置几头，数日始谢，房亦渐长 - "取置几头" means placing them on the table, "数日始谢" means they withered after several days, and "房亦渐长" means the room also gradually grew. The phrase describes the placement and subsequent changes of the flowers.
  • 剖之各得实五枚，如所来数 - "剖之" means cutting them open, "各得实五枚" means each producing five seeds, and "如所来数" means as many as the original number. The phrase describes the result of cutting open the flowers.
  • 茂即书其异，托侍女以报采 - "茂即书其异" means Mao immediately wrote about the unusual event, "托侍女" means entrusting the servant girl, and "以报采" means to report back to Cai. The phrase describes the act of recording and reporting the event.
  • 并蒂之谐此其征矣 - "并蒂之谐" means the harmony of twin blossoms, "此其征矣" means this is the sign. The phrase suggests that the twin blossoms are a sign of harmony or good fortune.
  • 因以朝鲜茧纸作鲤鱼，函两面俱画鳞甲，腹下令可以藏书，遂寄茂以诗 - "因以朝鲜茧纸作鲤鱼" means using Korean silk paper to make a carp, "函两面俱画鳞甲" means the envelope had scales drawn on both sides, "腹下令可以藏书" means the belly was designed to hold a letter, and "遂寄茂以诗" means then sent a poem to Mao. The phrase describes the creation and sending of a poetic message.
  • 吾怜子也 - "吾怜子也" means I pity you. The phrase reiterates the sentiment of sympathy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 并蒂花 - Twin blossoms, often associated with good fortune and harmony in Chinese culture.
  • 青莲子 - Lotus seeds, which are often used in Chinese literature to symbolize purity and spiritual enlightenment.

  • 一子聪俊 - Means 'one son is intelligent and handsome.'
  • 尤工文词 - Means 'especially skilled in writing.'
  • 不敢启齿 - Means 'dare not speak up.'
  • 开塾于家 - Means 'open a school at home.'
  • 负笈而至 - Means 'come with a bag of books.'
  • 仪容清雅 - Means 'demeanor is clean and elegant.'
  • 举止端详 - Means 'actions are dignified and proper.'
  • 窃念曰 - Means 'secretly think and say.'
  • 东床之选 - Refers to a chosen husband, derived from the story of Wang Xizhi.
  • 郑重 - Means 'serious and careful.'
  • 忍死以待 - Means 'wait patiently even if it means enduring hardship.'
  • 时值重阳令节边 - Means 'it is the time of the Double Ninth Festival.'
  • 满城风雨寂寥天 - Means 'the whole city is filled with wind and rain, making the sky desolate.'
  • 可怜不带登高兴 - Means 'pitifully, he does not have the mood to climb high.'
  • 孤负黄花又一年 - Means 'wasting another year of chrysanthemums.'
  • 花月空照两人边 - Means 'the moon and flowers are shining on two people.'
  • 安得团圆共一天 - Means 'how can we be together on the same day.'
  • 可惜风流人未会 - Means 'it is a pity that the talented person has not met.'
  • 错教乌兔送青年 - Means 'mistakenly letting the sun and moon take away youth.'
  • 趋出相见 - Means 'quickly go out to meet.'
  • 不回避 - Means 'not to avoid.'
  • 彼此交会 - Means 'meet each other.'
  • 其札甚恭 - Means 'their courtesy is very respectful.'
  • 家君馆谷之意 - Means 'my father's intention to provide shelter and food.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 同里曹姓者 - Refers to a man named Cao from the same village.
  • 张固垂意于彼 - Refers to Zhang Guo, who has an interest in the man named Cao.
  • 东床之选 - Refers to the story of Wang Xizhi, where he was chosen as a son-in-law because he was calm and composed.
  • 重阳令节 - Refers to the Double Ninth Festival, a traditional Chinese festival celebrated on the ninth day of the ninth month of the lunar calendar.
  • 黄花 - Refers to chrysanthemums, which are often associated with the Double Ninth Festival.
  • 花月 - Refers to the moon and flowers, often used in poetry to symbolize beauty and romance.
  • 乌兔 - Refers to the sun (乌) and the moon (兔), often used in Chinese poetry to symbolize the passage of time.

Proper Nouns

  • 同里曹姓者 - The name of the protagonist, Cao Bi.
  • 张固 - The name of the person who has an interest in Cao Bi.
  • 丽春 - The name of the woman who secretly observes Cao Bi.
  • 重阳令节 - The Double Ninth Festival.
  • 黄花 - Chrysanthemums.
  • 花月 - The moon and flowers.
  • 乌兔 - The sun and the moon.

[1365]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 翌日 - The next day, a common temporal marker in classical Chinese.
  • 命侍兒蘭香持彩笺 - Ordered the maid Lanxiang to hold a colored note, a formal way of commissioning someone to deliver a message.
  • 作詞一阕以寄生 - Composed a poem to send to the male protagonist, where '一阕' refers to a single poem (typically a Ci poem).
  • 清朝幔云，翠幕香凝 - A poetic description of a morning scene, with '清朝' meaning 'early morning' or 'clear morning,' '幔云' as 'curtained clouds,' and '翠幕香凝' as 'emerald curtain with fragrant condensation.'
  • 罗帏梦杳 - Silken curtain dreams vanish, a poetic way of saying dreams are distant or unreachable.
  • 深闺翡翠衾寒 - In the secluded chamber, the emerald quilt is cold, a metaphor for loneliness and emotional coldness.
  • 可是一春惟悴 - Indeed, the entire spring has been withered, a metaphor for the protagonist's emotional exhaustion.
  • 倦倚栏杆 - Weary of leaning on the railing, indicating a sense of fatigue and despair.
  • 最怪好花无主 - Most strange that the beautiful flower has no master, a metaphor for beauty without a possessor or love.
  • 狂蜂浪蝶几翩翻 - Mad bees and reckless butterflies flutter about, a metaphor for aimless or chaotic romantic pursuit.
  • 伤情处，枝头杜宇，血泪成丹 - In sorrowful moments, the cuckoo on the branch weeps blood, turning into vermilion, a poetic image of deep sorrow and emotional pain.
  • 荡荡游丝舞飞絮 - The drifting silk threads dance with flying catkins, a metaphor for the transience of beauty and emotion.
  • 奈芳心牵引，更有多般 - Alas, the tender heart is drawn in many ways, expressing the complexity of emotional entanglements.
  • 叹香销玉减，愁锁朱颜 - Lamenting the fading fragrance and diminishing jade, a metaphor for the loss of youth and beauty due to sorrow.
  • 望赤绳系足，定应合浦珠还 - Looking for the red thread to bind the feet, surely the pearl from HePu will return, a reference to the myth of the red thread of fate and the legend of the pearl returning to HePu (a place in Guangdong).
  • 洞房内，红摇花烛，鱼水同欢 - In the bridal chamber, red candles flicker, and husband and wife share joy, a poetic description of marriage and intimacy.
  • 喜不自胜 - Overcome with joy, an idiom expressing extreme happiness.
  • 审知女有相从之意 - Realizing the woman had intentions to follow him, a formal way of expressing that the male protagonist understood her romantic interest.
  • 吟诗一律 - Composed a regulated verse poem, a type of classical Chinese poetry with strict tonal and structural rules.
  • 书以复之 - Wrote it to reply, indicating the act of composing a response.
  • 曲栏深处遇娇姿 - In the deep corners of the curved railing, I met a delicate beauty, a romantic description of meeting a woman.
  • 一日相思十二时 - A day of longing spans twelve hours, a poetic way of expressing continuous yearning.
  • 自是琴中逢卓女 - It is as if I met Zhuo Nu in the music, a reference to the legend of Zhuo Nu, a woman of great beauty and talent.
  • 何须画里见崔徽 - Need I see Cui Hui in a painting? a reference to the famous beauty Cui Hui and the tradition of painting her likeness.
  • 绳牵丝幕应留意 - The red thread and silk curtain should be noted, a metaphor for the red thread of fate and the need to be attentive.
  • 腹袒东床定有期 - Exposing one's chest on the eastern bed, surely there will be a time, a reference to the story of Wang Xizhi (the 'East Bed') and his marriage proposal.
  • 昨夜嫦娥降消息，广寒已许折高枝 - Last night, Chang’e descended with news, and the Cold Palace has already agreed to pluck the high branch, a poetic reference to the moon goddess Chang’e and the metaphor of marriage or union.
  • 衷情悒怏 - Deep feelings of melancholy and frustration, expressing inner sorrow and emotional conflict.
  • 延入寝室，揖逊而坐 - Invited her into the bedroom and sat in a respectful manner, a formal description of a polite and courteous meeting.
  • 效唐人，作回文四时词 - I imitate the Tang people in composing a four-seasons poem in reverse script, referring to the classical Chinese 'huiwen' (回文) poetry, which can be read forwards and backwards.
  • 请君改教 - Please instruct me and correct it, a polite request for feedback.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 丽春 - A female character, likely a courtesan or a woman of refined taste, as the narrative suggests romantic and poetic engagement.
  • 兰香 - A servant or concubine, as implied by her role in delivering the note.
  • 彩笺 - A colorful note or letter, a common medium for romantic correspondence in classical literature.
  • 词 - A form of classical Chinese poetry known as *ci*, often set to music and with a fixed tonal pattern.
  • 杜宇 - The cuckoo bird, often associated with sorrow and longing in Chinese poetry.
  • 赤绳系足 - A mythological reference to the red thread that binds the feet of two people destined to be together, symbolizing fate and love.
  • 合浦珠还 - A reference to the legend of the pearl returning to HePu, a metaphor for reunion or restoration.
  • 洞房 - The bridal chamber, a traditional term for the wedding night.
  • 鱼水同欢 - A metaphor for the harmony and intimacy between husband and wife, derived from the relationship between fish and water.
  • 诗一律 - A regulated verse poem, specifically a *lüshi* (律诗), a classical form with strict tonal and structural rules.
  • 卓女 - A reference to Zhuo Nu, a legendary beauty and musician from the Han dynasty.
  • 崔徽 - A famous beauty from the Tang dynasty, often depicted in paintings and poetry.
  • 东床 - A reference to Wang Xizhi, whose 'eastern bed' (东床) was a term used to describe a suitor or groom.
  • 嫦娥 - The moon goddess, central to Chinese mythology, often associated with longing and separation.
  • 广寒 - The 'Cold Palace,' the name of the moon palace in Chinese mythology where Chang’e resides.
  • 折高枝 - A metaphor for marriage or union, as in 'plucking the high branch' from a tree.
  • 回文 - A type of classical Chinese poetry that can be read forwards and backwards, often used to express deep emotion and poetic skill.
  • 四时词 - A poem that reflects the four seasons, often used to express the passage of time and emotional change.

[1366]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其一 - Heading indicating the first stanza or section.
  • 花枝几朵红垂槛 - Describes red flowers hanging over the railing. '垂槛' (hanging over the railing) is a poetic way to describe flowers drooping over a fence or balcony.
  • 柳树千丝绿绕堤 - Describes green willow branches swaying along the riverbank. '绿绕堤' (green around the dike) suggests the willows encircling the water's edge.
  • 鸦鬓两蟠乌袅袅 - Describes the dark, curly hair of a woman, '鸦鬓' (crow-black hair) and '乌袅袅' (swaying like a crow) are poetic descriptions of hair.
  • 径苔行步印香泥 - Describes the soft, fragrant mud where footsteps leave marks. '香泥' (fragrant mud) is a poetic way to describe the ground.
  • 其二 - Heading indicating the second stanza or section.
  • 高梁画栋栖双燕 - Describes a high beam with painted rafters where two swallows are resting. '高梁画栋' (high beams with painted rafters) refers to a well-decorated house.
  • 叶展荷钱小叠青 - Describes small, green lotus leaves. '荷钱' (lotus coins) is a poetic term for young lotus leaves.
  • 腰细褪裙罗带缓 - Describes a slender waist with a loose silk belt. '罗带' (silk belt) is a traditional accessory.
  • 销魂暗泪滴围屏 - Describes tears falling on the screen. '围屏' (screen) is a decorative partition.
  • 其三 - Heading indicating the third stanza or section.
  • 明月晚天清皎皎 - Describes a clear, bright moon in the evening sky. '皎皎' (bright and clear) emphasizes the moon's brilliance.
  • 凛霜晴露冷悠悠 - Describes the cold, clear dew on a frosty morning. '凛霜' (frosty) and '冷悠悠' (cold and serene) create a sense of chill.
  • 情伤暗想闲长夜 - Describes the sadness felt during a long, quiet night. '暗想' (secretly thinking) and '闲长夜' (long, idle night) convey a sense of introspection.
  • 泪血垂胸锁恨愁 - Describes tears and blood flowing from the heart, symbolizing deep sorrow. '泪血' (tears and blood) is a hyperbolic expression of intense emotion.
  • 其四 - Heading indicating the fourth stanza or section.
  • 天冷雪花香堕指 - Describes cold, fragrant snowflakes falling. '香堕指' (fragrant and falling) suggests the snow's delicate and fragrant nature.
  • 日寒霜粉冻凝腮 - Describes the cold, frosty air freezing the cheeks. '霜粉' (frost powder) and '冻凝腮' (freezing the cheeks) create a vivid image of cold weather.
  • 悬悬意想空吁气 - Describes the feeling of suspense and the act of sighing. '悬悬' (suspenseful) and '空吁气' (sighing in vain) convey a sense of longing.
  • 夜月闲庭一树梅 - Describes a solitary plum tree in a quiet courtyard under the moonlight. '闲庭' (quiet courtyard) and '一树梅' (one plum tree) create a serene scene.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 花枝 - Refers to beautiful flowers, often associated with spring and beauty.
  • 柳树 - Willows are often associated with parting and sorrow in Chinese poetry.
  • 鸦鬓 - Crow-black hair, a common poetic description of dark, curly hair.
  • 径苔 - A path covered with moss, often used to describe a quiet, secluded place.
  • 高梁画栋 - High beams with painted rafters, indicating a well-decorated house.
  • 荷钱 - Young lotus leaves, often used to describe the early stages of growth.
  • 罗带 - Silk belts, traditional accessories worn by women.
  • 围屏 - Decorative screens, often used in traditional Chinese homes.
  • 明月 - The full moon, often associated with beauty and completeness.
  • 凛霜 - Frost, often associated with cold and winter.
  • 雪花 - Snowflakes, often used to describe the beauty and delicacy of snow.
  • 霜粉 - Frost powder, a poetic term for the fine, white frost that forms on surfaces.
  • 梅 - Plum trees, often associated with resilience and beauty in Chinese culture.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This line is a parallel structure, describing two different scenes: red flowers hanging over the railing and green willow branches swaying along the riverbank.
  • This line describes the appearance of a woman's hair and the soft, fragrant ground where footsteps leave marks.
  • This line describes a well-decorated house with swallows resting on the beams and small, green lotus leaves.
  • This line describes a slender woman with a loose silk belt and her tears falling on the screen.
  • This line describes the clear, bright moon in the evening sky and the cold, clear dew on a frosty morning.
  • This line describes the sadness felt during a long, quiet night and the deep sorrow expressed through tears and blood.
  • This line describes the cold, fragrant snowflakes falling and the cold, frosty air freezing the cheeks.
  • This line describes the feeling of suspense and the act of sighing, and a solitary plum tree in a quiet courtyard under the moonlight.

[1368]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 佳人才子是前缘 - 佳人 (beautiful woman) and 才子 (talented man) are paired to suggest a predestined connection or karmic bond.
  • 不作天仙作水仙 - 天仙 (heavenly immortal) and 水仙 (water nymph) are used to contrast celestial and earthly realms, suggesting a preference for a more grounded existence.
  • 白骨不埋黄壤土，清魂长浸碧波天 - 白骨 (white bones) and 黄壤土 (yellow soil) refer to the burial ground, while 清魂 (pure spirit) and 碧波天 (azure waves of heaven) suggest a spiritual existence in the afterlife.
  • 生前曾结同心带，死后仍开并蒂莲 - 同心带 (soul-bonded belt) and 并蒂莲 (twin lotus) are used to symbolize a deep, enduring bond between two people.
  • 千古风流千古恨，恩情不断藕丝牵 - 风流 (charm) and 恨 (regret) are paired to express the complex emotions of love and loss, while 藕丝 (lotus thread) is a metaphor for an unbreakable connection.
  • 诗词成帙，名之曰《并蒂莲集》，至今传诵不绝 - 成帙 (compiled into a collection) and 传诵不绝 (continuously recited) indicate the lasting popularity of the poetry collection.
  • 秋千会记 - 秋千会 (swing gathering) refers to a social event where women play on swings and enjoy each other's company.
  • 孛罗以故相齐国公子，拜宣徽院使 - 孛罗 (Bolu) is a name, 故相 (former chancellor) and 齐国公子 (prince of Qi) indicate his noble lineage, and 宣徽院使 (minister of the Bureau of Imperial Household) is his official title.
  • 奢都刺为佥判，东平王荣甫为经历 - 奢都刺 (Shadu Ci), 东平王荣甫 (Prince of Dongping Rongfu) are names, and 佥判 (vice-minister) and 经历 (counsellor) are their respective titles.
  • 私居后有杏园一所，取“春色满园关不住，一枝红杏出墙来”之意 - 杏园 (apricot garden) is a place, and the quote 春色满园关不住，一枝红杏出墙来 (the spring scenery cannot be confined, a branch of red apricot blooms over the wall) is a famous line from a poem by Ye Shaoweng.
  • 设秋千之戏，盛陈饮宴，欢笑竟日 - 设秋千之戏 (setting up swing games), 盛陈饮宴 (lavishly prepared banquets), and 欢笑竟日 (laughing all day) describe the festive atmosphere.
  • 各家亦隔一日设馔，自二月末至清明后方罢 - 设馔 (preparing meals), 自二月末至清明后方罢 (from the end of February to after Qingming Festival) indicate the duration and frequency of the gatherings.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 适枢密同金帖木耳不花子拜住过园外 - Describes a specific event involving 拜住 (Bayizu) visiting an outer garden. '适' means 'happened to' or 'just as.'

  • 潜于柳阴中窥之 - '潜' means 'secretly,' and '柳阴' means 'willow shade.'
  • 睹诸女皆绝色 - '睹' means 'saw,' and '诸女皆绝色' means 'all the girls were extremely beautiful.'
  • 遂久不去 - '遂' means 'then,' and '久不去' means 'remained for a long time.'
  • 为阁者所觉 - '为阁者' means 'the guard,' and '所觉' means 'became aware.'
  • 走报宣徽 - '走报' means 'ran to report,' and '宣徽' is a title, likely referring to a high-ranking official.
  • 索之亡矣 - '索之' means 'sought for,' and '亡矣' means 'was gone.'
  • 拜住归，具白于母 - '具白' means 'reported in detail,' and '于母' means 'to his mother.'
  • 母解意 - '解意' means 'understood the meaning.'
  • 乃遣媒于宣徽家求亲 - '乃' means 'then,' '遣媒' means 'sent a matchmaker,' and '求亲' means 'to seek marriage.'
  • 宣徽曰：“得非窥墙儿乎？” - '得非' means 'is it not,' and '窥墙儿' means 'the boy who peeped over the wall.'
  • 吾正择婿，可遣来一观 - '择婿' means 'choosing a son-in-law,' and '可遣来一观' means 'you can send him to be seen.'
  • 若果佳，则当许也 - '若果' means 'if indeed,' '佳' means 'good,' and '则当许也' means 'then we will agree.'
  • 媒归报同佥 - '媒归' means 'the matchmaker returned,' and '报同佥' means 'reported to the same official.'
  • 饰拜住以往 - '饰' means 'prepared,' and '以往' means 'to go.'
  • 宣徽见其美少年，心稍喜 - '心稍喜' means 'felt somewhat pleased.'
  • 但未知其才学 - '但' means 'but,' and '未知其才学' means 'did not know his talent and learning.'
  • 试之曰 - '试之' means 'tested him,' and '曰' means 'said.'
  • 尔喜观秋千，以此为题，《菩萨蛮》为调，赋南词一阕，能乎？ - '尔喜观秋千' means 'you like watching swings,' '以此为题' means 'use this as a topic,' '《菩萨蛮》为调' means 'with 'Pusa Man' as the tune,' '赋南词一阕' means 'compose a southern verse,' and '能乎' means 'can you?'
  • 拜住挥笔，以国字写之 - '挥笔' means 'wrote with a flourish,' and '以国字写之' means 'wrote it in Chinese characters.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遂獻《獨鲤朝天赋》 - 遂 means 'then' or 'thereupon,' indicating a sequence of events. 献 means 'to present' or 'to submit.' 《獨鲤朝天赋》 is a title of a work, which should be kept as is unless further clarification is needed.
  • 擢居清要 - 擢 means 'to promote' or 'to elevate,' and 清要 refers to important and prestigious positions. This phrase indicates promotion to a high-ranking position.
  • 出为夔州节度使 - 出 means 'to be sent out' or 'to be appointed,' and 节度使 is a military governor. This phrase indicates being appointed as the military governor of Kuizhou.
  • 既领夔州 - 既 means 'after' or 'having,' and 领 means 'to take charge of' or 'to govern.' This phrase indicates taking charge of Kuizhou.
  • 穆王已薨 - 已 means 'already,' and 薨 means 'to die' (for a noble). This phrase indicates that the Mu Wang had already died.
  • 其子希范继立 - 其子 means 'his son,' and 继立 means 'to succeed' or 'to ascend the throne.' This phrase indicates that his son, Xi Fan, succeeded him.
  • 因致书于希范 - 因 means 'therefore' or 'thus,' and 致书 means 'to send a letter.' This phrase indicates sending a letter to Xi Fan.
  • 叙畴昔入蜀之由 - 叙 means 'to narrate' or 'to recount,' 畴昔 means 'in the past' or 'formerly,' and 入蜀之由 means 'the reason for entering Shu.' This phrase indicates recounting the reasons for entering Shu in the past.
  • 仍以衡宗族为托 - 仍 means 'also' or 'furthermore,' 衡 means 'to balance' or 'to weigh,' and 宗族 means 'clan' or 'family.' This phrase indicates also entrusting the balancing of the clan.
  • 希范得书大惭 - 得书 means 'received the letter,' and 大惭 means 'greatly ashamed.' This phrase indicates that Xi Fan received the letter and was greatly ashamed.
  • 彬之亲友悉免其赋役 - 悉 means 'all' or 'completely,' and 免其赋役 means 'exempted from taxes and labor duties.' This phrase indicates that all of Bin's relatives and friends were exempted from taxes and labor duties.
  • 遂与瑞卿偕老焉 - 遂 means 'then' or 'thereupon,' 偕老 means 'to grow old together,' and 焉 is a particle that can be translated as 'there' or omitted in English. This phrase indicates that Bin then grew old with Ruiqing.
